commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual owner review is needed because the remaining blocker is the public Plugin SDK contract and claude-cli upgrade semantics, not a narrow autonomous repair.; Cleared: The diff changes TypeScript runtime/types, tests, docs, and generated SDK API metadata only; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, package scripts, or artifact download/execute paths. (sha=a1516cc21518622639e89e245116280f4a802953)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `ownsNativeCompaction` to CLI backend plugins, wires it through backend resolution, marks `claude-cli` as backend-owned, skips OpenClaw compaction for owning non-harness CLI sessions, and documents/tests the contract.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +217, Docs +42. Total +284 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature/API PR, though the changed branch has deterministic tests and PR-body live logs from a lowered-budget real `claude-cli` run.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual owner review is needed because the remaining blocker is the public Plugin SDK contract and `claude-cli` upgrade semantics, not a narrow autonomous repair.

target.title: feat(agents): generalized native compaction ownership for CLI backends
target.body:
## Summary

Generalizes native-compaction ownership for CLI backends via a new `ownsNativeCompaction` capability flag.

When a CLI backend runs an agent that compacts its **own** transcript (e.g. Claude Code's internal auto-compaction) and the session has **no** native-harness compaction endpoint, OpenClaw's CLI compaction lifecycle returns a **no-op** instead of running its safeguard summarizer — it defers to the backend rather than fighting the backend's own compaction or hard-failing the turn.

- The capability is **generalized** — any CLI backend can declare `ownsNativeCompaction: true`. This PR wires it for **`claude-cli`**: Claude Code compacts internally and exposes no harness endpoint, so the lifecycle returns a no-op.
- Codex continues to route to its native-harness compaction endpoint (matched by the session's `agentHarnessId`); the no-op applies **only** when there is no harness endpoint.

(Scope note: this PR is the generalized mechanism + the `claude-cli` declaration only. Opting in any other specific backend is out of scope here.)

## What changed since the last review

This PR is **purely the opt-out.** The earlier `contextTokens` cap-precedence change was removed — once a backend owns compaction, its budget never drives OpenClaw compaction, so capping below the native window solved a problem that no longer exists. As a result `src/agents/context.ts` and `src/agents/context.test.ts` are **byte-identical to base** (no GA-1M behavior change; existing GA-window tests pass untouched). The Plugin SDK API baseline was also refreshed for the new `ownsNativeCompaction` field (`docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`).

## Testing

- `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts` deterministically exercises the lifecycle: over-budget session with `ownsNativeCompaction` + no harness endpoint → no-op defer; native-harness sessions (e.g. Codex) still route to the harness endpoint; non-owning backends still run the safeguard summarizer.
- `src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts` covers capability registration.
- Affected suite green locally (140 tests).

## Live proof (after-fix)

Captured on a clean build of this PR, plain `claude-cli` backend, with the session context budget lowered to **500 tokens** (the lowered-budget defer path). A real `claude-cli` turn — note the actual `claude` binary invocation in `cli argv` — exceeds the budget, and OpenClaw returns the **no-op defer** instead of running its safeguard summarizer:

```
[agent/cli-backend]     cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=claude-opus-4-6 promptChars=48 trigger=user useResume=false session=none
[agent/cli-backend]     cli argv: claude -p --output-format stream-json … --model claude-opus-4-6 --session-id <redacted>
[agents/cli-compaction] CLI backend "claude-cli" owns native compaction — deferring to backend
```

The budget was lowered to 500 for the capture because GA opus/sonnet report a 1M-token window (so a real session is never *organically* over budget), and a bare `claude-cli` `contextTokens` overlay is dropped by the config loader — this is the lowered-budget path. The deterministic before/after (compacts without the flag / defers with it) remains in `cli-compaction.test.ts`.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
